subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: The topic is communicated accurately and effectively, with comprehensive contextâsetting and clear definitions of key terms.
The research question is precisely formulated, well-focused and consistently linked back to the analysis.
A wide range of relevant methods and sources is employed, with clear evidence of informed selection and an explicit acknowledgement of limitations.
weaknesses: Minor lapses in scope definition (ambiguity over exact end date) slightly weaken the precision of focus.
Occasional digressions into non-course models reduce methodological clarity.
The tools paragraph omits the name of one model, which detracts from full transparency of method selection.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates strong alignment between question, context and methods. Explanations are accurate and well-justified, and the methodology shows clear evidence of informed selection. Occasional imprecision prevents a perfect mark, but the work clearly belongs at the top of the 5â6 band.

strengths: Sources are generally applied appropriately, with data tables and figures drawn from primary filings and peer-reviewed literature integrated to support claims.
Subject knowledge is clear and coherent, with accurate use of core concepts such as the marketing mix, PESO model, and profitability ratios.
Terminology is mostly accurate and consistent, reflecting solid command of disciplinary language.
weaknesses: Some source applications are descriptive rather than critical, and occasional reliance on less rigorous media evidence weakens academic rigor.
A few concepts (e.g., margin labelling) are treated superficially, suggesting incomplete mastery.
Minor inaccuracies in the application of certain terms obscure meaning for a non-expert reader.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay shows a coherent grasp of business-management concepts and applies authoritative sources appropriately. Inconsistencies in critical engagement with evidence and occasional superficial treatment of financial details limit the work to the upper range of the 3â4 band.

strengths: Research is broad and clearly relevant, drawing on SEC filings, scholarly articles, industry reports and competitor benchmarks.
Analysis employs multiple frameworks (SWOT, PESO, ratio benchmarking) and links quantitative and qualitative evidence to the research question.
Discussion is well-developed, with critical evaluation of tool limitations, acknowledgment of data gaps, and integration of alternative explanations.
weaknesses: Critical evaluation occasionally remains at a surface level (e.g., noting âuncertain ROIâ without deeper financial estimation).
Causality is seldom probed beyond correlation, limiting the sophistication of some conclusions.
Some popular media sources are used where more rigorous evidence would strengthen the argument.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidate delivers excellent research that is consistently relevant to the research question, combining sophisticated analysis with well-supported conclusions. While deeper critique of causality and more rigorous source selection could elevate the work further, the overall performance lies solidly in the 10â12 band, meriting a score of 10 within the constraints of the total mark.

strengths: The structure adheres fully to EE requirements, with a clear title page, contents, logically ordered sections and a complete bibliography.
Layout is clean and reader-friendly: figures and tables are captioned, pages are numbered, and formatting is consistent throughout.
The essay meets all presentation expectations, facilitating easy navigation and comprehension.
weaknesses: A few minor formatting glitches (incomplete sentence, under-formatted mission statement quotation) interrupt flow.
Some figure captions are overly verbose, and a chart lacks axis labels and legends, slightly reducing clarity.
A mid-paragraph text omission disrupts readability in two places.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is uniformly strong, satisfying all structural and formatting criteria. Minor lapses in completeness and figure clarity prevent a flawless execution, but the overall organisation and layout justify the top mark in the 3â4 band.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Clear, focused formulation.** The research question is explicitly presented in bold at the start and revisited in the introduction. It contains the firm, the variable (digital marketing to build brand equity), the dependent outcome (profitability) and a defined period, meeting IB expectations.

**Connection to discussion.** Each analytical section (marketing mix, PESO, ratios) repeatedly links findings back to the stated question, demonstrating deliberate alignment. While occasional digressions (e.g., affordability in mission statement) appear, they are brief and still tied to profitability.

**Focus assessment.** Because the study keeps returning to âto what extentâ and treats profitability as the evaluative lens, the strand fits the top band. Minor breadth issues (multiple tools sometimes expand beyond marketing) prevent the maximum, so a score of 5 is appropriate.

**Argument development.** The essay progresses logically from context to tool-based analysis and synthesises findings in a conclusion that weighs multiple factors. Each major section contains internal evaluations (e.g., limitations of SWOT, PESO), showing reflective thinking.

**Evaluation quality.** The student critically evaluates data lags, acknowledges inability to prove causation, and considers legal threats and affordability issues that temper the positive narrative. These reflections are integrated rather than appended, strengthening the discussion.

**Limitations.** Evaluation sometimes remains surface level (e.g., stating âuncertain ROIâ without financial estimation) and could integrate counter-evidence more rigorously. Nonetheless, the argument is well-developed with partially critical evaluation, matching the top of the 4-6 band; hence a score of 6.

**Clarity and coherence of knowledge.** The student demonstrates a sound grasp of business-management concepts such as marketing mix, PESO, SWOT, profitability ratios and CSR, applying them coherently to Airbnbâs context. Definitions are accurate and usually embedded smoothly within the discussion.

**Effective use of sources.** Financial data from SEC filings underpin profitability analysis, and scholarly work (e.g., Wichmann et al., Haryanto & Retnaningrum) grounds theoretical claims. However, some sections veer into general commentary or rely on popular media pieces, diluting academic rigour.

**Verdict.** The breadth of knowledge is clear, yet occasional superficial treatment of finance concepts (e.g., mis-labelled margins, limited exploration of causality) suggests there is room for deeper mastery. This fits the upper end of the 3-4 band; a score of 4 is appropriate.

**Terminology usage.** Core business-management termsâbrand equity, asset-light model, dynamic pricing, net profit marginâare employed frequently and largely correctly. The glossary of tools (SWOT, PESO, CRAAP) is used appropriately, signalling familiarity with disciplinary language.

**Consistency and precision.** While the majority of terms are used accurately, a few slips occur (e.g., conflating âsales revenueâ with âsalesâ, minor inconsistency in margin labelling). Some jargon from sources is quoted without full clarification, which occasionally obscures meaning for a non-expert reader.

**Overall assessment.** Terminology is mostly accurate and relevant, though not always precise or critically dissected. This aligns with the âmostly accurateâ descriptor of the 3-4 band; the higher mark of 4 reflects the generally consistent command of concepts.

**Depth and structure of analysis.** The student applies multiple analytical frameworks (marketing mix, SWOT, PESO, ratio benchmarking) and interprets financial data to build arguments linking digital marketing to profitability. Connections between marketing expenditure and lagged profit margins show analytical thinking beyond simple description.

**Support for conclusions.** Many assertions are backed by numerical evidence (e.g., year-on-year margin changes post-marketing spend) and literature. The candidate also recognises alternative explanations such as post-pandemic recovery, showing awareness of complexity.

**Areas for improvement.** The analysis sometimes stops at correlation without probing causation, and weighting of factors (e.g., regulatory threats vs. viral campaigns) remains qualitative. Despite these limitations, the work exhibits clear, well-supported analysis, justifying a high mark within the 4-6 band; 6 is awarded.

**Relevance and integration.** A large number of current, authoritative sources (SEC filings, peer-reviewed journals, industry reports) are quoted and referenced throughout the analysis, generally supporting claims effectively. Data tables, charts and figures are created from primary company filings, demonstrating appropriate application.

**Limitations.** At times, sources are used descriptively rather than critically, and some assertions (e.g., impact of UI/UX complexity on older users) rely on anecdotal or blog evidence without triangulation. There is limited synthesis of conflicting viewpoints, and in a few places the link between a citation and the claim is weak.

**Overall judgment.** The application is mostly relevant and appropriate but not consistently rigorous across the essay, aligning best with the 3-4 band; the higher mark of 4 is awarded for the generally solid, though not unerringly critical, application.

**Range and relevance of sources/methods.** The essay draws on SEC filings, scholarly journals, textbooks, industry analyses and company communications, showing an ample spread of secondary data appropriate to the research aim. Use of quantitative (profitability ratios, benchmarking) and qualitative tools (SWOT, PESO, Marketing Mix) illustrates methodological variety.

**Evidence of informed selection.** The student justifies choosing secondary data for reliability and cites CRAAP evaluation. The decision to benchmark against Expedia and to focus on net profit margin is explained, indicating thoughtful methodological choices.

**Limitations acknowledged.** The candidate openly recognises weaknesses (e.g., inability to isolate causation, lack of pre-IPO data). Overall, the methodology is appropriate and clearly justified, meriting the 5-6 band; a score of 5 reflects strong but not exemplary execution.

**Breadth and relevance.** The investigation draws on an extensive set of current, authoritative and varied sourcesâSEC filings, competitor reports, scholarly articles and industry dataâdemonstrating clear relevance to the research question.

**Effective selection and deployment.** Key quantitative data (marketing spend, profit margins) and qualitative materials (mission statements, press releases) are deliberately chosen to illuminate the relationship between digital marketing, brand equity and profitability. Source triangulation is evident in most sections, showing critical selection rather than indiscriminate compilation.

**Limiting factors.** Some popular media items of lesser academic weight are included, and the essay could have benefited from deeper primary data or independent financial modelling. Nonetheless, the overall research base is clearly connected and adequate for a top-half score within the 7-9 band; a score of 7 is merited.

**Formal presentation.** The student includes a correctly formatted contents page, consistent heading hierarchy, labelled figures/tables, numbered pages and a complete bibliography in a consistent citation style. Word count is provided and within limit.

**Visual clarity.** Diagrams and charts are captioned, sourced and referred to in the text. The overall layout is clean, with appropriate spacing and font choices that enhance readability.

Because all layout and formatting expectations are demonstrably fulfilled, the submission attains the highest band for this strand; a score of 4 reflects the absence of significant flaws.

**Structural organisation.** The essay follows the required EE format: title page with word count, table of contents, introduction, methodology, main body divided by analytical tools, conclusion, bibliography and appendices. Headings and sub-headings guide the reader effectively.

**Flow and coherence.** Within sections, paragraphs generally build logically, and cross-referencing to figures and appendices is clear. Minor redundancies (e.g., duplicate mission/vision commentary) do not materially disrupt flow.

Given that all structural requirements are met and facilitate reader understanding, the work sits comfortably in the 3-4 band; the upper score of 4 is awarded.

annotations.comment: The discussion of pricing ends mid-sentence, indicating text is missing; this disrupts readability and coherence.
Promotion section critically notes absence of public ROI data, showing awareness of data limitations.
This paragraph cites a strong correlation study but does not critically assess causality or acknowledge alternative explanations for revenue recovery.
SWOT weaknesses rely heavily on anecdotal media sources; consider supplementing with empirical quality-control studies for rigor.
Evaluation of used tools is comprehensive but would benefit from prioritizing tool impact on profitability metrics.
Introduction provides coherent business context and effectively integrates multiple authoritative sources to frame Airbnbâs digital marketing journey.
Mission statement is under-formatted: quotation marks and capitalization are inconsistent with academic style.
The critique linking discrimination scandals to credibility loss is incisive, demonstrating critical evaluation of brand messaging.
The Marketing Mix tool is introduced with clear rationale, linking controllable variables to brand equity and profitability analysis.
The PESO model is appropriately chosen to segment digital channels, enhancing the multifaceted analysis of marketing spend.
The localization initiative is correlated with revenue growth, yet alternative factors such as market rebound are not critically weighed.
The title succinctly communicates the organization, variable of interest, and timeframe, providing clear focus and context for the investigation.
The research question is precisely formulated, specifying firm, variable, evaluative lens, and period, demonstrating strong alignment with the studyâs aim.
Word count is clearly stated and within the 4 000-word limit, meeting formatting requirements.
Contents page is comprehensive and accurately lists sections with corresponding page numbers, aiding navigation.
Sentence ends abruptly and is incomplete, disrupting flow. The introduction requires revision to finish this thought.
Limitation of the profitability ratios tool is candidly stated, exposing challenges in isolating digital marketing spend from overall marketing expenditure.
Figure caption is descriptive but the alt text is overly verbose. Condense alt description for clarity and conciseness.
Vision statement analysis correctly identifies lack of affordability messaging, showing nuanced conceptual understanding.
Methodology clearly justifies use of secondary data sources and applies the CRAAP test, demonstrating informed selection.
The tools paragraph mentions inclusion of a non-course model without naming it here. Specify all chosen tools to maintain clarity and focus.
Limitation of the Marketing Mix is critically acknowledged, indicating awareness of the toolâs inability to capture consumer feedback perception.
Theoretical framing of mission and vision statements demonstrates sound subject knowledge and appropriate application of source material.
Opportunities section uses credible market growth forecasts, demonstrating effective application of source material.
Threats are described qualitatively; quantifying the financial impact of legal restrictions would strengthen evaluation.
Fig 4âs alt text is overly detailed; a concise caption with key insights would improve reader focus.
Formula for net profit margin is correctly formatted in LaTeX, enhancing mathematical clarity.
Comparative analysis with Expedia is well-structured, revealing meaningful contrasts in profit margin volatility.
Evaluation paragraph summarizes findings but would benefit from direct reference to quantitative data for greater impact.
SWOT strengths section effectively integrates quantitative and qualitative data to support claims about user engagement and cost efficiency.
PESO Model introduction accurately describes origin and purpose, showing strong disciplinary knowledge.
Justification for selecting net profit margin as the primary metric is clear and aligns with research focus.
Marketing Mix definition is accurately explained with proper citation, reflecting clear use of terminology.
Fig 2 lacks axis labels and legend for bar charts, reducing clarity of the comparative profitability visualization.
Earned media analysis cites view counts but omits conversion or engagement metrics; recommend integrating campaign performance data.
Shared media section ends mid-paragraph, suggesting missing content; revise to complete the argument.
Evaluation of the PESO model correctly highlights the trade-off between channel reach and measurable ROI.
Evaluation notes limitation of subsidiary data dilution; suggest disaggregating segments for a more precise comparison.
Bar chart figure lacks axes labels and legend; include these to ensure data interpretation without ambiguity.
The conclusion of data analysis acknowledges seasonal and macroeconomic factors, showing reflective critical thinking.
Bibliography entries exhibit inconsistent citation styles and formatting; align all references to a single style guide.
